pattern: With only a handful of nodes, every possible set of branches to keep can be enumerated directly, and for each kept set a fresh shortest-path computation restricted to just those nodes tells you whether every pair still stays within the allowed distance.
hints: The number of branches is tiny, so consider trying every possible subset of branches to keep (or, equivalently, to close) rather than looking for a clever formula.

For a candidate set of kept branches, distances must be recomputed using only roads between branches that are still open -- a path cannot pass through a closed branch, so build a fresh distance table for just that subset.

A subset is valid exactly when every pair of kept branches has a shortest distance (within the induced subgraph) no greater than the limit; sets of size zero or one are automatically valid, and counting the valid subsets over all 2^n possibilities gives the answer.

edge_inputs.description: statement-style triangle with one cheap edge and two expensive ones
statement-style triangle with parallel edges between the same pair
two branches joined by an edge exactly at the distance limit
two branches joined by an edge one unit over the distance limit
duplicate roads between the same pair, only the cheaper one should matter
ten branches in a chain with a large distance limit so almost everything qualifies
ten branches in a chain with a distance limit too small for the full chain to qualify

stress.description: random connected graphs over up to ten branches with occasional parallel roads
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = min(10, max(2, (size % 9) + 2))
    maxDistance = rng.randint(1, 100000)
    order = list(range(n))
    rng.shuffle(order)
    roads = []
    for i in range(1, n):
        u = order[i]
        v = order[rng.randint(0, i - 1)]
        w = rng.randint(1, 1000)
        roads.append([u, v, w])
    extra = rng.randint(0, 5)
    for _ in range(extra):
        u = rng.randint(0, n - 1)
        v = rng.randint(0, n - 1)
        if u == v:
            continue
        w = rng.randint(1, 1000)
        roads.append([u, v, w])
    return [n, maxDistance, roads]
stress.note: a random spanning tree guarantees full connectivity, with a few extra (possibly parallel) roads layered on top
